filter_index.video_title: Beas Kund Trek | A Great Trek Right Out Of Manali | Best Time | Difficulty | Indiahikes

snow_or_rain_notes: Snow present at Palchhani Thatch and on trek in mid-June, traces of winter lingering
Snow patches on the ground in summer (mid-June to mid-July)
Some snow on final ascend to Beas Kund in early July, most snow melted by mid-July
Snow gone from mid-August to September
Heavy rain in the afternoon from mid-July to mid-August; carrying a poncho is compulsory
temperature_notes: 4 warm layers recommended across all three windows; cold subject to wind, moisture and other factors

terrain_challenges: First two days are relatively easy
Third day (Lady Leg Day) includes a steep ascent and an equally steep descent in one day
Trail disappears in meadows on many occasions, creating a risk of getting lost
Unexpected snow patches in certain sections in June can make the trek trickier
Trail can get very slushy when it rains heavily
altitude_or_weather_risks: Rapid altitude gain on day one (above 9,000 ft to above 10,000 ft) can be strenuous
At altitudes above 10,000 ft, risk of Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS) is real
AMS can affect first-time trekkers as well as experienced trekkers
Even fit persons climbing too high too soon are at equal risk of AMS
Best done during monsoon months (July, August, September); rain is part of the experience

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog or run 5 km in under 35 minutes (minimum recommended fitness level)
Build cardiovascular endurance and muscle strength
Train core, calves, glutes, quadriceps, and hamstrings
approval_requirements: Screenshots must demonstrate meeting fitness criteria at least 3 times in the past month
prep_window: 15 to 20 days before trek date for screenshot upload

experience_themes: Frozen Beas Kund surrounded by snow in mid-June, ice begins to thaw as July descends
Snow patches on the ground in summer despite it being summer
Meadows emerge as monsoon season deepens
Frozen Beas Kund melting in small concentric circles in mid-July, ice thinning, meadows around lake emerging past mid-July
Lush greenery replacing snow from mid-August to September
Beas Kund flaunts deep royal blue colour with green grass and flowers blooming around it in mid-August to September
primary_watchouts: Snow patches on the ground in mid-June to mid-July despite summer
Heavy afternoon rain and compulsory poncho from mid-July to mid-August
Frequent rains and unannounced clouds shrouding lake and trail at monsoon peak (mid-July to mid-August)
Cold is subject to wind, moisture and other factors; 4 warm layers recommended
First two days are relatively easy
Third day (Lady Leg Day) includes a steep ascent and an equally steep descent in one day
best_for: Catching arresting views of underrated mountains of Himachal Pradesh under blue skies (mid-August to September)
Experiencing lush meadows and royal blue Beas Kund with blooming flowers (mid-August to September)
Seeing frozen Beas Kund surrounded by snow (mid-June)
not_ideal_for: Trekking solo if not well versed in the region
Travelling to Manali on the same day as starting the trek (due to rapid altitude gain)
